headline: AI Memory Lands Its Record Nasdaq Debut
deck: SK Hynix priced its US listing at $149 a share and raised about $26.5 billion, the largest ever by a foreign company on Wall Street. It began trading in New York today; the bet underneath is the high-bandwidth memory bolted to almost every AI accelerator.

art.caption: The memory that sits beside the AI accelerator now trades in New York; the listing prices the compute cycle, not a single chip.

body: SK Hynix priced its American depositary shares at $149 and raised roughly $26.5 billion, a sale its bankers call the largest-ever US equity debut by a foreign company [E1]. The offering ran about seven times oversubscribed, and the chipmaker began trading on Nasdaq today under the temporary ticker SKHYV, which converts to SKHY on 13 July [E1][E2].

Scale here tracks a single product. SK Hynix supplies the high-bandwidth memory that sits next to the processors inside the data centres of the largest AI buyers, and it holds the majority of that market by revenue [E1]. Investors are not buying a bet on one model or one accelerator; they are buying the memory layer the whole build-out runs on [E1].

The plumbing around the listing filled in fast. Underwriters priced 177.9 million shares, and an ETF issuer has already filed leveraged funds, SKUU and SKDD, timed to launch on 13 July, the first session after the ticker settles [E2]. That is the mark of an issue the market wants to trade, not merely hold [E2].

Timing is the argument. A record memory-chip listing cleared its book in a week when oil jumped on the Gulf and rate-cut hopes cooled, which says AI-memory demand is being priced as more durable than the macro chill around it [E1][E2]. Capital is funding the supply side of compute at scale, and doing it into a nervous tape.

The caution sits inside the deal. Oversubscription is appetite, not performance, and the first session's close will land after this edition goes to press, so the debut's verdict is still open [E1]. The near-term discriminators are plain, where SKHY settles against its $149 offer and whether high-bandwidth-memory pricing holds through the year. A record book buys attention, not a result [E1][E2].

evidence_box.fragment: SK Hynix lists on Nasdaq today at $149; raised about $26.5 billion; largest-ever US equity debut by a foreign company; about seven times oversubscribed
SK Hynix hits Nasdaq July 10 under temporary ticker SKHYV converting to SKHY on 13 July; 177.9m shares; leveraged ETFs SKUU and SKDD expected 13 July
